channel.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories
channel.description: Top stories in the U.S. and world news, politics, health, science, business, music, arts and culture. Nonprofit journalism with a mission. This is NPR.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

image.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories

item.title: Australian police uncover 3 tons of cocaine
item.description: The drug was found on June 19 in plastic tubs buried in bunkers hidden beneath three shipping containers. Two Sydney residents were arrested. They face potential sentences of life in prison.
item.content:encoded:
The drug was found on June 19 in plastic tubs buried in bunkers hidden beneath three shipping containers. Two Sydney residents were arrested. They face potential sentences of life in prison.

(Image credit: Australian Federal Police via AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: China hits back at U.S. sanctions restricting its exports to American defense firms
item.description: China on Monday announced sanctions on 10 American military-related companies in response to a recent U.S. move that bars some leading Chinese tech companies from defense contracts.
item.content:encoded:
China on Monday announced sanctions on 10 American military-related companies in response to a recent U.S. move that bars some leading Chinese tech companies from defense contracts.

(Image credit: Mark Schiefelbein)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: A larger World Cup had some worrying the competition would suffer. It hasn't
item.description: This year's edition of the FIFA World Cup continues to be thrilling with unexpected performances from tournament first-timers and surprising results from some of the favorites.
item.content:encoded:
This year's edition of the FIFA World Cup continues to be thrilling with unexpected performances from tournament first-timers and surprising results from some of the favorites.

(Image credit: Chandan Khanna)
item.dc:creator: Russell Lewis

item.title: Colombia Election: Right-wing Abelardo de la Espriella wins, initial count shows
item.description: Colombia wakes up to a sharp political turn to right as Abelardo de la Espriella's preliminary victory redraws the country's path on security, economy, and peace.
item.content:encoded:
Colombia wakes up to a sharp political turn to right as Abelardo de la Espriella's preliminary victory redraws the country's path on security, economy, and peace.

(Image credit: Rodrigo Abd)
item.dc:creator: John Otis

item.title: 'House of the Dragon,' Season 3, Episode 1: Number one with a gullet
item.description: In the Season 3 premiere, a new dragon enters the chat and the Battle of the Gullet is in full swing.
item.content:encoded:
In the Season 3 premiere, a new dragon enters the chat and the Battle of the Gullet is in full swing.

(Image credit: Ollie Upton)
item.dc:creator: Glen Weldon

item.title: Trump threatens to 'hit Iran very hard again' while Vance is in Switzerland for talks
item.description: President Trump has threatened further attacks on Iran while Vice President Vance attended talks with Iranian officials in Switzerland on Sunday.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump has threatened further attacks on Iran while Vice President Vance attended talks with Iranian officials in Switzerland on Sunday.

(Image credit: Nathan Howard)
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster

item.title: Ukrainian attacks prompt Russian-held Crimea to halt civilian gasoline sales
item.description: Officials in Russia-occupied Crimea suspended civilian gasoline sales Sunday as Ukraine ramped up attacks on fuel supplies on the Black Sea peninsula.
item.content:encoded:
Officials in Russia-occupied Crimea suspended civilian gasoline sales Sunday as Ukraine ramped up attacks on fuel supplies on the Black Sea peninsula.

(Image credit: Andriy Andriyenko/Ukrainian 65 Mechanized brigade)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump claims vandals damaged D.C. Reflecting Pool, and says it will be drained again
item.description: The president posted on Truth Social claiming vandals slashed the pool's lining and poured chemicals into the water, saying arrests have been made. He provided no evidence for his claims.
item.content:encoded:
The president posted on Truth Social claiming vandals slashed the pool's lining and poured chemicals into the water, saying arrests have been made. He provided no evidence for his claims.

(Image credit: Mark Schiefelbein)
item.dc:creator: Willem Marx

item.title: Colombia runoff vote shaped by security fears and conflict warnings
item.description: Voters head to a runoff in Colombia Sunday between candidates offering sharply different approaches to armed groups, with the frontrunner calling for intensified military action over peace talks
item.content:encoded:
Voters head to a runoff in Colombia Sunday between candidates offering sharply different approaches to armed groups, with the frontrunner calling for intensified military action over peace talks

(Image credit: Joaquin Sarmiento)
item.dc:creator: John Otis

item.title: Adrian Chiarella's "Leviticus" shows that horror's final boys can find love
item.description: Homophobia lives, and stalks, in Adrian Chiarella's debut feature.
item.content:encoded: Homophobia lives, and stalks, in Adrian Chiarella's debut feature.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Benk
